title: pisum arvense var. leptolobum
intro: Pisum arvense var. leptolobum, also known as the garden pea or field pea, is a perennial legume native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include being a natural antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including aiding in digestion, reducing fever, and relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The herbal preparations of Pisum arvense var. leptolobum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
